Entries.UsedFor: Primary authoritative source for all API facts: auth headers, pagination model, incremental watermark params, write operations (POST/PATCH/DELETE), response-only codes (200/204), API versioning (URL path /v2/), page size default (10), universal PK convention (integer 'id' field across 51/53 schemas with id).
Entries.Excerpt: OpenAPI 3.0.1 spec for OpenWater API 2.0. 92 paths. All 46 GET operations use pageIndex (0 by default) / pageSize (10 by default). Auth headers X-ClientKey (required:true) + X-ApiKey (required:true) on all 92 paths. Response codes: only 200 and 204 documented — no error schema defined in components.schemas.

Entries.UsedFor: Rate limit specification: 3 calls/sec free tier, paid tiers at 5/10/25+. Auth header scheme confirmation. API key provisioning path.
Entries.Excerpt: Rate limit: 3 calls/sec (free tier). Paid tiers: 5, 10, 25+ calls/sec. API access requires $2000/yr. API keys provisioned via System Settings > Permissions > API Keys.

Entries.UsedFor: BatchRequestWaitTime: 3 calls per 1-second window from the rate limit spec = 1000ms window.
Entries.Excerpt: Rate limit is documented as 3 calls/sec (1-second window). BatchRequestWaitTime = 1000ms to model the 1-second rolling window.

Entries.UsedFor: Configuration.AuthFlow: api-key dual-header scheme (no OAuth). X-ClientKey and X-ApiKey headers required on all 92 endpoints. X-OrganizationCode optional.
Entries.Excerpt: All operations carry header parameters: X-ClientKey (required: true, description: 'Specify the client key'), X-ApiKey (required: true, description: 'Specify the api key'), X-OrganizationCode (not required, description: 'Specify the organization code'), X-SuppressEmails (not required). No securitySchemes defined — no OAuth flow.

Entries.UsedFor: Configuration.AuthHeaderPattern: two required headers per all 92 operations.
Entries.Excerpt: Header name='X-ClientKey' in='header' required=true; Header name='X-ApiKey' in='header' required=true. Present on every operation in the spec.

Entries.UsedFor: Configuration.PaginationDefaults: offset pagination via pageIndex (default 0) + pageSize (default 10). Uniform across all 46 GET collection endpoints. PagingInfo.totalCount + numberOfPages + hasNextPage for traversal.
Entries.Excerpt: pageIndex: 'Page index (0 by default)', pageSize: 'Page size (10 by default)'. PagingInfo schema: {pageIndex, pageSize, totalCount, numberOfPages (readOnly), hasPreviousPage (readOnly), hasNextPage (readOnly)}. Consistent across all 28 paginated collection GETs.

Entries.UsedFor: Configuration.IncrementalSyncCapability: multiple doors expose timestamp filter params (lastModifiedSinceUtc, createdSinceUtc, mostRecentTransactionSinceUtc, lastModifiedUtc, deletedSinceUtc).
Entries.Excerpt: Applications: lastModifiedSinceUtc. Users: lastModifiedSinceUtc. Sessions: lastModifiedSinceUtc. Evaluations: lastModifiedSinceUtc. Programs: createdSinceUtc. Invoices: mostRecentTransactionSinceUtc. BillingLineItems/Payments/Refunds: lastModifiedUtc. DeletedApplications/Sessions: deletedSinceUtc.

Entries.UsedFor: Configuration.WebhooksAvailable: false. No webhook registration or subscription endpoints in the spec.
Entries.Excerpt: 92 paths enumerated. No /webhooks, /subscriptions, /events, or any push-subscription endpoint exists in the spec. The API is strictly pull-only at the protocol level.

Entries.UsedFor: Configuration.BulkOperationsAvailable: false. All write operations are per-record (single POST/PATCH/DELETE). No batch endpoints documented.
Entries.Excerpt: 38 POST, 14 PATCH, 12 DELETE operations — all single-record. No /batch, /bulk, or array-body write endpoint in the spec.

Entries.UsedFor: Configuration.APIVersioningStrategy: URL-path versioning via /v2/ prefix on all 92 paths.
Entries.Excerpt: All 92 paths begin with /v2/. spec.info.version = '2.0'. No version header or Accept-header versioning.

Entries.UsedFor: Configuration.ErrorResponseShape: only 200 and 204 status codes documented across all 110 operations. No error response body schema defined.
Entries.Excerpt: Response codes found across all operations: ['200', '204']. No 400/401/403/404/429/500 responses documented. components.schemas contains 0 error/problem schemas. Error shape is therefore undocumented in the spec — connector must handle non-2xx at the transport level.

Entries.UsedFor: Configuration.WriteCapability: POST/PATCH/DELETE operations are present — API supports create, update, delete on multiple resource families.
Entries.Excerpt: 38 POST endpoints (Applications, Sessions, Users, JudgeAssignments, JudgeTeams, Media, etc). 14 PATCH endpoints (Evaluations, Users, Sessions, JudgeAssignments, ScheduleItems, etc). 12 DELETE endpoints (Applications, Sessions, JudgeAssignments, SessionChairs, ScheduleItems, etc).

Entries.UsedFor: Configuration.ConcurrencyControl: none. No If-Match, ETag, version, or conditional update headers documented anywhere in the spec.
Entries.Excerpt: 92 paths examined. No ETag header, no If-Match header, no version field in request schemas, no conditional update mechanism documented.

Entries.UsedFor: Configuration.DeleteSemantics: hard delete (DELETE endpoints return 200/204 with no tombstone) + vendor-side tombstone doors for Applications and Sessions (GET /v2/Applications/DeletedData, GET /v2/Sessions/DeletedData returning deletedAtUtc).
Entries.Excerpt: DELETE /v2/Applications/{id} responses: 200/204 (no body on 204). Models.DeletedApplication.DeletedApplicationListItemModel has deletedAtUtc field. Models.Program.ProgramListItemModel + Models.Fund.FundListItemModel have isArchived boolean (soft-archive pattern for Programs and Funds, separate from hard delete).

Entries.UsedFor: Configuration.universalPK: 'id' field as integer appears in 51 of 53 schemas with an id field. The 2 string-id schemas are FieldValues/Form utility schemas, not core syncable objects.
Entries.Excerpt: 51/53 schemas with 'id' field type integer. Examples: ApplicationListItemModel.id (integer, 'Application id'), UserListItemModel.id (integer), SessionListItemModel.id (integer), EvaluationListItemModel.id (integer), ProgramListItemModel.id (inferred from DetailsResponse.id). String-id schemas: Models.FieldValues.RowModel (uuid), Models.Form.ListValue (uuid) — both are form-metadata utility types, not syncable record sets.

Entries.UsedFor: Configuration.NavigationBaseURL: tenant domain pattern. The spec URL (https://api.secure-platform.com/swagger/v2/swagger.json) shows the base domain; tenants use https://<tenant>.secure-platform.com.
Entries.Excerpt: Spec source URL per manifest.json: 'https://api.secure-platform.com/swagger/v2/swagger.json'. The tenant domain convention is <tenant>.secure-platform.com — the X-ClientKey header identifies the specific tenant. No explicit servers[] block in the spec.

Entries.UsedFor: Configuration.OutOfScopeObjectFamilies: Media (upload-only, no list), SessionChairs (write-only, no list GET), Export (async job triggers, not record sets), Account/Authenticate (auth action), Jobs (single-item status poll), Organizations/TimeZone (singleton lookup).
Entries.Excerpt: Media: POST /v2/Media + GET /v2/Media/{id} only — no list GET. SessionChairs: POST + PATCH + DELETE only — zero GETs. Export: all POST-only async job triggers. Account: POST /v2/Account/Authenticate only. Jobs: GET /v2/Jobs/{id} — single-item only. Organizations: GET /v2/Organizations/{organizationId}/TimeZone — singleton config.

Entries.UsedFor: Amendment round 2: JudgeAssignment CreateIDLocation body->n/a. POST /v2/JudgeAssignments/Round responses {200,204} carry no content/schema (no record id in body); DELETE passes judgeUserId+roundId as query params (no requestBody). n/a is the sentinel forcing connector override of CreateRecord/DeleteRecord per connector-code-conventions.md.
Entries.Excerpt: /v2/JudgeAssignments/Round post.responses {200:{description:Success},204:{description:Assigned successfully}}; delete.parameters [judgeUserId(query),roundId(query)]; delete has no requestBody.

Entries.UsedFor: Amendment round 2: JudgeAssignment DeleteIDLocation body->n/a. POST /v2/JudgeAssignments/Round responses {200,204} carry no content/schema (no record id in body); DELETE passes judgeUserId+roundId as query params (no requestBody). n/a is the sentinel forcing connector override of CreateRecord/DeleteRecord per connector-code-conventions.md.
Entries.Excerpt: /v2/JudgeAssignments/Round post.responses {200:{description:Success},204:{description:Assigned successfully}}; delete.parameters [judgeUserId(query),roundId(query)]; delete has no requestBody.

Entries.UsedFor: Product nature from the vendor site + connector source-study
Entries.Excerpt: OpenWater is an awards / grants / abstracts management platform

Entries.UsedFor: Default API host hard-coded as DEFAULT_BASE_URL in the OpenAPI-documented v2 surface
Entries.Excerpt: https://api.getopenwater.com (OpenWater Public API v2 base)

Entries.UsedFor: OpenWater authenticates with the X-ClientKey + X-ApiKey header pair; matches the seeded "OpenWater API" credential type
Entries.Excerpt: ClientKey / ApiKey header pair (OpenWater API credential type)
